title: Dog Vomit Looks Like Coffee Grounds: An Urgent Care Guide
meta_desc: If your dog's vomit looks like coffee grounds, it is a sign of internal bleeding. Learn why this is a high-priority veterinary emergency.
content_html:
TL;DR: Vomit that resembles coffee grounds is a sign of digested blood and constitutes a life-threatening veterinary emergency. You should take a photo of the material and transport your pet to an emergency clinic immediately to address potential internal bleeding or organ failure.

What does it mean if my dog's vomit looks like coffee grounds?

Finding vomit that resembles dark, grainy coffee grounds can be very frightening for a pet owner. This appearance is a medical sign of digested blood. When blood is exposed to stomach acid, it oxidizes and turns a dark brown or black color, clumping into the textured appearance you are seeing. This indicates that bleeding has occurred somewhere in the upper gastrointestinal tract, such as the stomach or the start of the small intestine.

Is coffee ground vomit a veterinary emergency for my pet?

  • Seek immediate emergency veterinary care, as the urgency level for this symptom is high and it cannot be monitored at home.
  • Be aware that this symptom often points to serious underlying conditions such as deep stomach ulcers, ingestion of toxins (like rat poison), heavy parasite loads, or organ failure.
  • Act quickly to prevent life-threatening complications like rapid blood loss, shock, and severe anemia.

How does taking a photo of the vomit help the veterinary triage team?

  • Take a clear photograph of the vomit before cleaning it up to provide a visual record for the medical team.
  • Show this photo to the veterinary triage team so they can confirm the presence of digested blood and assess the severity of the situation.
  • Provide objective evidence of the volume and consistency, which helps the team prioritize your dog's care.
  • Enable the medical staff to prepare the necessary diagnostic tools before you even enter the exam room.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

In dogs, vomitus that appears as coffee grounds suggests the presence of digested blood, often originating from a bleeding lesion such as a gastric ulcer or neoplasm. Potential causes of vomiting with digested blood include dietary indiscretion, ingestion of drugs or toxins (e.g., NSAIDs, corticosteroids), systemic illnesses (e.g., pancreatitis, hypoadrenocorticism), endoparasitism, or bacterial/viral infections. If hematemesis or melena are present, or if the animal is systemically unwell, a specific diagnosis should be sought through clinicopathologic testing and diagnostic imaging.

Chapter: Gastroenterology, Infectious Disease

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 156)

faqs.question: Could this just be something my dog ate?
faqs.answer: While some dark foods can mimic this look, you should always treat coffee-ground-textured vomit as digested blood until proven otherwise by a professional. It is better to be safe than to delay treatment for internal bleeding.

faqs.question: What are other symptoms of internal bleeding to watch for?
faqs.answer: In addition to the vomit, look for pale gums, lethargy, weakness, a racing heartbeat, or black, tarry stools (melena). These are all signs of a critical medical situation.

faqs.question: What will the vet do to help my dog?
faqs.answer: The vet will likely perform a physical exam, blood work, and possibly imaging like an X-ray or ultrasound to find the source of the bleeding and stabilize your dog with fluids or medication.
